ps140: You fully brought yourself to Him who gave you perfect life,* O most blessed one,* as a living and animate fruitful offering,* as a well-pleasing and most pure sacrifice.* Therefore, you have become a well-pleasing intercessor,* rescuing from the tempest all those who hymn you with faith, O martyr,** and call upon you, O Theodore.

In your pure soul you diligently cultivated the seed* that the Word sown everywhere,* increasing it through the pangs of your suffering,* and wisely storing it in the granaries of heaven,* you found incorruptible delight,* in which you now rejoice, O blessed one.** By your intercessions before Christ, save those who faithfully hymn you.

O martyr and passion-bearer of Christ,* by your prayers save those in various tribulations,* casting out every evil siege,* driving away soul-destroying grief,* and beseeching mercy and grace for us.* So that, saved by your prayers,** we may joyfully hymn your honored struggles, O Theodore.
4, Podoben “As one valiant among the martyrs…”

The Holy Spirit showed you to be* a courageous athlete* and invincible warrior.* You cast down the attacker* by the wisdom of your words* and by the patient manifestation of your deeds, O wise one.* Therefore, you have received crowns of victory* and have been joined to the choirs on high,** O great martyr Theodore.

You were a godly pillar of piety,* O most rich one,* disgusted by the temples of the impious.* You were led as a most perfect lamb,* a well-pleasing sacrifice* to Him Who was blamelessly sacrificed for you,* and Who glorified your holy memory* and, as a treasury of miracles for those in the world,** He gifted you, O Theodore.

Lifted up upon a cross,* your flesh lacerated* and wounded with sharp arrows,* stretched upon a tree,* cut with swords,* and beset with various devices of torture,* you proved yourself unyielding and invincible* by the power of Him who was nailed to the Cross,** O Theodore, glory of the martyrs.

Today the honored memory of Christ’s passion-bearer* has shone forth more brightly than the morning star,* invisibly illumining the hearts of the faithful* and dispelling spiritual clouds* by the grace of the Spirit.* To him let us cry aloud, O lovers of the martyrs:* O divinely bestowed grace revealed to the faithful,* pouring forth a multitude of miracles upon those who flee to you,* O blessed Theodore!* As you unceasingly entreat Christ,* do not fail to grant that those who faithfully honour your memory** obtain eternal blessings.
stavrotheotokion: Seeing her Lamb hastening to the slaughter,* the Ewe-Lamb followed Him eagerly, crying aloud:* “Where are You going, O Christ, my sweetest Child?* O longsuffering One, why do You proceed so swiftly and without delay?* O Jesus most-desired, O sinless One, O Lord, rich in mercy!* Speak to me, Your handmaiden, O my well-beloved Son!* Do not pass in silence by me, who beget You in an uncommon manner,** O all-compassionate God, who grant great mercy to the world.”
aposticha: O faithful, let us honour and fittingly bless* the namesake of God’s gifts and the heir of His blessedness* the great and valiant martyr Theodore,* the champion of the universe,** for he entreats Christ God for our souls.
stavrotheotokion_aposticha: “I cannot bear, O my Child,* to behold You  Who grants vigilance to all,* sleeping upon the Tree,* in order to grant divine and salvific vigilance* to those who of old were lulled into the sleep of death* by the fruit of transgression,”** thus said the Virgin, weeping, whom we magnify.
